items.title: Function templates: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? A function template is one runtime function that inspects an argument's dynamic type.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A function template is instantiated at compile time into type-specific functions after deduction and constraint checking.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A function template is instantiated at compile time into type-specific functions after deduction and constraint checking.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

items.title: Function templates: executable application
items.prompt: Define and use a function template larger that returns the greater of two const references, read three integers, and print the greatest by composing that template.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Start from input validation and the declared observable output, then apply only the target language rule before considering optimization.

feedback.correct: All visible and hidden deterministic behavior checks passed for the bounded source task.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic behavior check failed; inspect bounds, input state, and the target rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every normal and boundary behavior test.
Applies Function templates without undefined behavior or an out-of-scope workaround.
